name: South & Central Highlands Memorial Sites 9 days
duration: Places:Ho Chi Minh City - Long Tan Cross - Cu Chi Tunnels - Mekong Delta - Pleiku - Kon Tum
highlights: Visit Nui Dat Task Force Base, Long Tan Battle Cross, and the infamous Ia Drang Valley
Engage with veterans for personal insights into battles and their experiences
Enjoy a boat ride along Mekong’s floating markets and picturesque villages
Experience cable car rides to Ba Den Mountain and tours of Central Highlands

itinerary.title: Saigon Arrival
itinerary.description:
Morning /Afternoon

Having reported to Tan Son Nhat International Airport, a friendly tour guide will be waiting to greet you. From there, you will be transferred to your hotel for check-in. NOTE: 

The standard check-in time is 14:00, but if you wish to settle in earlier, an early check-in option is available for a surcharge. Please contact your travel consultant for arrangements. If, for any reason, you cannot locate the tour leader, do not hesitate to reach out to your travel consultant for assistance
itinerary.accommodation: Hotel in Ho Chi Minh City

itinerary.title: Long Tan - Nui Dat Battle Field Full Day
itinerary.description:
Morning

At 07:30, your tour guide will meet you at your hotel in Ho Chi Minh City to begin a full-day exploration. Your journey starts with a visit to Nui Dat Task Force Base, once the strategic headquarters for the Australian and New Zealand Army Corps (ANZAC) during the Vietnam War. At its height, this base housed 5,000 Australian troops, playing a crucial role in military operations. As you explore the remnants of the base, you will gain deeper insights into its significance, and there may even be opportunities to chat with Vietnam veterans who were part of this chapter in history. Later, ...
itinerary.accommodation: Hotel in Ho Chi Minh City

itinerary.title: Ho Chi Minh - Cu Chi - Tay Ninh - Ba Den
itinerary.description: After breakfast today, you will set out on an exciting excursion to Cu Chi Tunnels, a symbolic underground network stretching more than 200 km. These tunnels were of great importance during the Vietnam War, helping the Viet Cong control a large rural area just 30 - 40 km from Saigon, all the way to the Cambodian border. Get ready to discover different layers of passageways, which include nooks for sleeping, working, cooking, and even a hospital. You will also learn about clever survival strategies used by guerrilla fighters and even be able to experience what it was like to move via them yours...
itinerary.accommodation: Hotel in Ho Chi Minh City

itinerary.title: Ho Chi Minh - Mekong Delta - Dong Tam - Ap Bac
itinerary.description: The following day, you will travel by road down the mighty Mekong, making the first stop at Dong Tam Snake Farm in Vinh Long. Established on October 27, 1979, it sits in an area littered with mines and barbed wire left by the US Army in the past. At the farm, you will find yourself lost in a kingdom of hundreds of snake species. Many of them wind their way around branches. Besides learning about their characteristics and physiology, you can also take in the peaceful atmosphere of the nearby fruit gardens, which will remind you of the calm countryside
itinerary.accommodation: Hotel in in Ho Chi Minh City

itinerary.title: Ho Chi Minh to Pleiku Flight
itinerary.description: The journey goes on as you move out of Ho Chi Minh City and take a short flight to Pleiku, a region of great historical significance. When the plane soars over Central Highlands, you can see the breathtaking landscape of green forests and rolling mountains before landing in this once-strategic stronghold of the Vietnam War. Upon arrival, the atmosphere shifts, immersing you in a land deeply connected to wartime history, where stories of resilience and sacrifice come to life. Apart from that, the area captivates with its rugged beauty, presenting a balance between remembrance and appreciation o...

itinerary.title: Pleiku - Half-Day War Tour
itinerary.description: You start the morning by traveling to Komtum, where you will have a look at the former US DZ site, which still holds some remnants of French barracks. The day trip keeps up along a scenic road onto Pleime, and from Pleime Hill, you will delight in a gorgeous 2-hour view of the infamous la Drang Valley. This site is historically significant as the location of the first major battle between the Vietnam People Army and the US Army. It was from this place that both forces had their first direct confrontation, introducing new strategies and tactics that would influence not only the rest of the war ...

itinerary.title: Battlefield Tour in Kon Tum - Dak To - Tan Canh
itinerary.description: On this day, you will be driven deep into the casualty sites of Dak To and Tan Canh. The tour starts off with a transfer to Phoenix Airstrip, originally a key airbase for the 4th Infantry Division of the U.S. Army. It was selected to be the place for supplying troops and conducting operations in the Central Highlands. From there, you will venture out to Skull Hill (Hill 601), a strategically significant location that controlled access to both Kon Tum and Dak To in the past

itinerary.title: Pleiku to Ho Chi Minh Flight
itinerary.description: After having breakfast, you will take a morning flight from Pleiku to Ho Chi Minh City. On arrival at Tan Son Nhat International Airport, our driver will transfer you to your hotel in the city center. Once you have checked in and settled, the rest of the day is yours to explore at your own pace. You may want to stroll bustling streets, visit famous landmarks like Ben Thanh Market or Notre Dame Cathedral, or relax and soak in the vibrant energy of the largest city. At night, you will catch up on a sleep in your hotel here.
itinerary.accommodation: Hotel in Ho Chi Minh City

itinerary.title: Saigon Departure
itinerary.description: Now, it is time to say goodbye to the beautiful memories you have created throughout the tour. Following breakfast, let take in the vibe of Ho Chi Minh City for one last time before heading to the airport for your flight home. Wishing you a safe flight and hope to see you again soon.

included: Accommodation in TWN/ DBL sharing accommodation at hotel, except overnight on train and homestay (if any)
All transport (including airport pick up & see off), as per itinerary
Meals as indicated in the program
All in-tour stationed local English speaking guide.
Entrance fees for Vietnam attractions, as per itinerary
importantNotes: PROGRAMS & SCHEDULES SUBJECT TO CHANGE WITHOUT PRIOR NOTICE
